creator: jsatkinson@embarqmail.com
text:
Edwin Groothuis wrote:
> Maintainer of multimedia/vlc-devel,
> 
> Please note that PR ports/129282 has just been submitted.
> 
> If it contains a patch for an upgrade, an enhancement or a bug fix
> you agree on, reply to this email stating that you approve the patch
> and a committer will take care of it.
> 
> The full text of the PR can be found at:
>     http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=ports/129282
> 

This is generally correct. The affected version is NOT 0.9.5 though. 
0.9.5 was the release that addressed the issues. So the affected 
versions are effectively 0.9.0 through 0.9.4. I mentioned both of these 
CVEs in a follow up to ports/128359, which was the 0.9.5 submission.

FreeBSD moved from 0.9.0-test1 directly to 0.9.5, so it is possible that 
FreeBSD never included an affected version. I can't confirm this at this 
time because of being busy (holidays) and that there is no -test1 marked 
in their git for easy reference. However, I have no objections to 
documenting them to be complete/precise/safe.

It is also worth noting that 0.9.5 is vulnerable to other issues that 
have already been documented in vulnxml. I mention this to avoid any 
confusion. 0.9.5 is not "clean", it's just not affected by these CVEs 
specifically.

The official announcement from VideoLAN (that confirms the affected 
range) is here:
http://www.videolan.org/security/sa0809.html
